rationale: The one substantive ground-truth concern (dsocek's point that hardcoding component names is fragile and a single source of truth like the packed-modules mapping should drive discovery instead) is closely echoed by X's finding #1, which independently identifies the same fragmented-hardcoded-list problem across manager.py/offloader/registry.py and proposes deriving from a per-pipeline declaration. Y explicitly considers the exact same dedup question (registry.py:427 vs manager.py) during its sweep but concludes it's 'not warranted,' effectively rejecting the reviewer's actual suggestion rather than surfacing it. Y's findings (missing activation round-trip test, parametrize duplication) are grounded and actionable but orthogonal to what the human reviewers actually raised, giving it much lower recall despite thorough, well-cited validation work.
